meta_title: For Freelancers: user onboarding to Solve can't collaborate (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't collaborate. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: User Onboarding to Solve Can't Collaborate
content.seo_description: Master user onboarding to solve collaboration bottlenecks. A high-impact, intermediate guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale.
content.answer_block: To solve collaboration friction, implement a role-based user onboarding flow that maps stakeholder permissions to data access. By automating the invitation lifecycle and standardizing access control via Antigravity, you eliminate manual overhead, reduce project downtime, and provide clients with a professional, self-service environment that builds immediate trust.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is anxious, and your project is stalling because stakeholders are trapped behind a wall of manual access requests. You aren't just building an app; you are building a bottleneck. When collaboration breaks down, your reputation follows, turning what should have been a smooth delivery into a support-ticket nightmare.

But there is a better way. By treating user onboarding as a core architectural feature rather than an afterthought, you transform the entry point of your SaaS into a high-velocity collaboration engine. This guide shows you how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build an automated, professional onboarding system that impresses clients and saves you hours of manual configuration.

steps.title: Architect the Role-Based Access Schema
steps.description: Define clear user roles (Admin, Editor, Viewer) to prevent permission drift. This ensures every invited collaborator has the exact access they need to contribute immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SQL schema and TypeScript interface for a multi-tenant SaaS application that supports granular role-based access control (RBAC) with organization-wide invitations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate Prisma schema for User, Organization, and Membership models with RBAC support'

steps.title: Automate Invitation Workflows
steps.description: Remove the manual friction of adding users. Create a secure, tokenized invitation link system that automates account creation and permission assignment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a secure API endpoint for sending invitation emails, including logic to generate one-time use tokens and associate new users with their respective organizations upon registration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create an invitation service layer for sending emails and handling token verification'

steps.title: Implement Just-in-Time (JIT) Provisioning
steps.description: Ensure users have access the second they sign up. JIT provisioning bridges the gap between invitation and active collaboration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a middleware function that checks for pending organization memberships upon user login and automatically upgrades the user session with the correct organization context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement Next.js middleware to handle organization context injection for authenticated users'

steps.title: Build the Client Dashboard Onboarding UI
steps.description: A clean UI for managing users is essential. Build a self-service dashboard that allows your clients to invite their own team members without you.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a reusable React component for an 'Invite Team Member' modal, including form validation for email addresses and a dropdown for selecting user roles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate React/Tailwind modal component for team management'

steps.title: Stress-Test Access Policies
steps.description: Verify that your onboarding system handles edge cases, such as invite expiration and role escalation, to prevent security loopholes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the invitation flow, ensuring that expired tokens are rejected and that new users cannot access resources until their account is verified.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Write Jest test suite for invitation service edge cases'

pro_tips: Always use UUIDs for invitation tokens to prevent enumeration attacks.
Implement an 'Organization Switcher' UI pattern if your clients manage multiple projects.
Use rate-limiting on the invitation endpoint to prevent automated spam abuse.
Sync your onboarding flow with a CRM like HubSpot so you know exactly when a client adds their team.
Add a 'Pending' status to user memberships so admins can track who hasn't accepted their invite yet.
content.next_steps: Once the onboarding flow is live, audit your error handling logs to ensure no invitations fail silently. Then, document the process in a short 'How-To' PDF for your clients to reduce your support burden.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting time on manual user management and start scaling your freelance business with automated architectural patterns. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
